title: Why India's first hydrogen train is special - explained
content: With the launch of a hydrogen train, India has undoubtedly taken a big leap in the sphere of greener transportation.  India’s first hydrogen-powered train has 10 coaches, with the capacity to carry 2,600 passengers. It will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana.

title: 'Harassed by seniors': Teacher drives car into Godavari river, kills self and 2 kids
content: A teacher and his two children died after their car plunged into the Godavari river. Police suspect the tragic incident was a deliberate act of suicide. The teacher had posted a WhatsApp status alleging harassment by his seniors. Local residents alerted authorities, and rescue teams recovered the vehicle and bodies. An investigation is now underway into the allegations and the deaths.

title: A Chhatrapati kicked it off. Messi and Ronaldo rule Kolhapur now
content: Kolhapur celebrates football with giant player cutouts during the World Cup.  Local clubs, established decades ago, foster a strong footballing tradition.  These clubs rely on public donations and community support for their operations.  The city has hosted international matches and draws large crowds to local games.  Kolhapur's devotion to football continues to grow despite infrastructure limitations.

title: Ex-cricketer backs Rohit to sign off from ODIs with 'innings that people remember'
content: Patel said the India opener has repeatedly shown during his career that he responds strongly after a lean run and expects him to do so again if Sunday's match turns out to be his last.

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train update: Progress so far and Japan's E10 plan
content: India's first bullet train project between Mumbai and Ahmedabad is progressing well. Initial operations will commence using Indian high-speed trains in 2027. Japan's next-generation E10 Shinkansen trains will be introduced later, after 2030. The first operational stretch will connect Surat and Bilimora in Gujarat. This initiative significantly reduces travel time between the two major cities.

title: Amazon wants software, not supervisors, to decide warehouse staffing
content: Amazon is testing software that decides warehouse staffing, but internal documents and Slack chats obtained by Business Insider show managers overriding the AI, disabling it, and working around it. Some say the algorithm misreads the floor and recommends cuts that don't fit real conditions. Amazon plans hard enforcement in 2026, aiming to limit how far managers can deviate from its staffing recommendations.

title: Why Argentina President is skipping the World Cup final despite Trump's presence
content: Argentine President Javier Milei will not attend the World Cup final in New Jersey. He believes his superstition, known as cábalas, ensures Argentina’s victory. Milei will watch the match from his residence in Buenos Aires. Donald Trump and FIFA president Gianni Infantino are expected to attend the final. This decision follows a long-standing presidential tradition of avoiding crucial matches.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: 'Ghost font': The font humans can read but AI can't
content: An American designer has developed a unique font that humans can read. This innovative font relies on a motion-based optical illusion to display hidden text. Advanced artificial intelligence systems have struggled to decipher the concealed messages accurately. The creator believes this technology could enhance security measures like CAPTCHAs and watermarking. This development highlights a current difference in human and AI visual processing capabilities.

title: 58 beavers turned Mount St Helens' wasteland into a thriving wetland
content: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.

title: Meta 'admits' its AI detection tool can fail to identify cropped AI-generated photos
content: Meta's new AI image detection tool has limitations. The tool may fail to identify some AI-generated images when cropped. This disclosure follows a Reuters analysis of Meta's preview detection tool. Researchers highlight watermark-based detection's technical limitations with image modifications. Companies continue developing tools to identify AI-generated content amid growing concerns.

title: Your landlord sold the property—can the new owner evict you?
content: When a property is sold in India, a tenant's rental agreement does not simply disappear. The new landlord is bound by the original terms set by the previous owner. Rent hikes can only occur if specified in an escalation clause within the contract. Tenants who continue paying their old landlord post-sale remain protected, and their rights to the security deposit are preserved regardless of the ownership change.

title: LPG subsidy bill races beyond budget, may hit Rs 1 lakh crore this fiscal
content: LPG subsidy spending may surpass one lakh crore rupees in FY27. This projection significantly exceeds the budget's thirty thousand crore rupee allocation. Higher fuel prices and ongoing war uncertainty are driving this increased subsidy burden. Government finances face pressure from these rising subsidy commitments. Capital expenditure is expected to remain measured during the first half of FY27.

title: Sold for $4.9 mn, Pele's 1958 jersey becomes 2nd-most expensive; which is first?
content: Pelé, who died in 2022, was just 17 years old when he scored twice in Brazil's 5-2 victory over hosts Sweden in the World Cup final at Rasunda Stadium. He remains the youngest player to score in a World Cup final.

title: Ram Mandir donation theft: SIT seeks more time for probe, likely to submit report to SC
content: The Special Investigation Team probing Ram Temple donations seeks more time. An interim report will be submitted directly to the Supreme Court on Monday. The SIT was formed at the request of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. An earlier report led to eight arrests and recovery of nearly eighty lakh rupees. The Trust will meet on July twenty-second to discuss findings and measures.

title: 'Modi thinks way in advance': PM says Hormuz crisis didn't affect railways
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated Indian Railways' rapid electrification aids national energy security. Electrification over twelve years prevented a standstill during Middle East crises. Less than thirty percent of the rail network was electrified before 2014. Nearly ninety-nine percent of India's rail network is now electrified. This transformation ensures uninterrupted train operations despite global energy challenges.

title: Another hilltop murder: Andhra woman plots husband's killing with lover's help
content: A woman and her lover allegedly murdered her husband during a family pilgrimage. The victim was lured to a remote temple road and ambushed. His wife secretly tracked their location for her accomplices. The couple's young daughter was present during the brutal attack. Police arrested the wife, her lover, and two associates.

title: Kamaru Usman eyes one more run at UFC gold ahead of Dricus Du Plessis showdown
content: Kamaru Usman believes victory over former middleweight champion Dricus Du Plessis could move him one step closer to another UFC title. In an exclusive interview with TimesofIndia. com, the former welterweight champion reflects on his lengthy layoff, his pursuit of a second world title, the challenge posed by Du Plessis, why every fight now has to be meaningful, and what he wants people to remember about his career.

title: Niti Aayog's roadmap to turn India into $691 billion bioeconomy
content: NITI Aayog proposes six national initiatives for India's biotechnology sector. This roadmap aims for a $691 billion bioeconomy by the year 2035. These missions will strengthen capabilities in healthcare and agriculture. They also focus on industrial biotechnology and disease surveillance. The plan includes a Rs 50,000-crore growth fund.

title: State took land but didn't pay compensation for 50 years; Andhra Pradesh HC orders payment
content: A 72-year-old woman will receive land compensation awarded nearly fifty years ago. The Andhra Pradesh high court found the money never reached the landowner, despite the award. The court directed authorities to pay the original amount with 12 percent annual interest. This payment must be made within two months, along with other benefits. The state had argued the acquisition was lawful and notices were sent.

title: Quote of the day by Meryl Streep
content: Meryl Streep, an acclaimed actress, is celebrated for her versatile roles and impactful screen presence. Her career began with voice training and progressed through theatre and numerous film successes. Streep's iconic performances include roles in 'The Devil Wears Prada' and 'The Iron Lady'. She has amassed significant wealth and experienced personal relationships throughout her life. Her profound quote emphasizes how thoughts shape character and ultimately lead to destiny.

title: First time ever: FIFA unveils special 'ring' award ahead of Messi vs Yamal final
content: The FIFA World Cup champions will receive exclusive championship rings for the first time. These rings will debut after the 2026 final between Spain and Argentina. One side features the trophy, and the other reflects the winning nation's identity. A limited collection of 2,026 rings will be available, with 30 for winners. The final match features Lionel Messi facing Lamine Yamal in a captivating generational clash.

title: House hunting? These tips can help you avoid AI rental scams
content: In an era of advanced technology, online rental scams have become more cunning, leveraging artificial intelligence to deceive unsuspecting tenants. Fraudsters craft fake property listings and convincingly impersonate landlords. To safeguard against these scams, it's crucial for renters to be cautious of unusually low rents and landlords who refuse inspections. Always conduct in-person visits and confirm ownership before making any financial commitments, opting for secure payment methods to mitigate risks.

title: PM Modi flags off India's first hydrogen train; check route, timings & how it works
content: India’s first hydrogen train is a 10-coach passenger offering. According to Indian Railways, globally the few operational or under testing hydrogen trains have 2-3 passenger coaches, making India’s first hydrogen train the longest of its type in the world.

title: 'Threw sticks, tried to block us': Nashik family recounts scary 15-km chase by mob
content: A Nashik family faced terror after objecting to comments made at Bhavli dam. They were chased and attacked for nearly fifteen kilometers by a group of men. The family's ordeal began after a minor altercation escalated into a violent pursuit. An FIR was registered, and police have arrested twelve individuals involved in the incident. Security has been increased at Bhavli dam to ensure visitor safety.

title: E20 dispute: Maruti Suzuki to challenge order to replace car, cites fuel contamination
content: Maruti Suzuki will challenge a consumer court order directing a vehicle replacement. The court ordered the company to replace a Grand Vitara Strong Hybrid with a new E20-compatible model. The customer reported recurring technical issues after E20 petrol became widely available. Maruti Suzuki disputes the findings and claims the car was E20 compatible. The company plans to appeal the order before an appropriate higher forum.

title: Russian recruits survive just 20-30 minutes on Ukraine frontlines: CIA chief explains why
content: Russian soldiers face a brief battlefield life expectancy of minutes.  AI-powered drones have become specialized, low-cost killing machines on the front.  Ukraine's mastery of drone warfare acts as a significant equalizer in the conflict.  This technological advantage has helped Ukraine hold off Russia's superior force.  Both sides have experienced substantial casualties since the invasion began.

title: Falklands poster row: FIFA to probe Messi's Argentina over 'Malvinas' banner
content: FIFA is reviewing a banner displayed by Argentina players after their World Cup semi-final win. Players held a "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" message during celebrations following their victory. This gesture has led to a disciplinary review by FIFA's independent committee. Argentina's football association faced a similar fine in 2014 for the same banner. The team now prepares for the World Cup final against Spain.

title: PF for self-employed, workers in unorganised sector on way
content: Employees Provident Fund Organisation is developing a universal provident fund scheme. This new plan will cover millions in the unorganised sector and self-employed individuals. Subscribers can contribute daily or annually with tax benefits on their savings. The agency is studying global models to finalize the basic framework for this initiative. This self-financing scheme aims to extend retirement savings beyond the current EPFO network.

title: Gold price prediction: Is it time to buy gold on dips? Check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are favouring a buy on dips strategy, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities.

title: Several trapped after landslide buries homes in China's Chongqing
content: State broadcaster CCTV said a local community worker noticed rocks falling from a hillside at around 8 am local time and issued an emergency warning. Authorities immediately began evacuating more than 60 residents, but the landslide struck at 9.08 am, trapping several people before the evacuation could be completed. Footage aired by CCTV showed a section of the hillside collapsing onto a cluster of riverside homes.

title: Uganda bus crash: At least 21 killed after school trip ends in tragedy
content: A school bus overturned in eastern Uganda, killing twenty children and the school's founder. The tragic accident occurred Thursday evening after the vehicle reportedly lost control on a steep hill. Dozens of other pupils and staff sustained injuries in the devastating crash. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about school transport safety across the nation. Uganda faces thousands of road fatalities annually due to various contributing factors.

title: India coach gives blunt 'no bridge needed' response on Kohli-Gambhir rift
content: Ahead of the first ODI at Edgbaston, Kohli was seen batting in the nets while Gambhir watched from close range without speaking to him. Kotak, meanwhile, was spotted having a conversation with the former India captain.

title: Dhamaal 4' BO Day 8 [LIVE]: Ajay Devgn's comedy inches towards Rs 100 crore
content: Ajay Devgn starrer 'Dhamaal 4' has wrapped up its first week with an impressive Rs. 96 crore India net, while its worldwide gross has climbed to Rs 132.74 crore. As the film enters Day 8 and into its second weekend, all eyes are on whether the comedy entertainer can cross the coveted Rs 100 crore India net mark despite competition from Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey.'

title: Salima Tete to lead India at Hockey WC; England, China await in pool stage
content: The squad, currently training at the national camp in Bengaluru, combines experienced players with youngsters who have impressed in recent international competitions.

title: Karnataka government urged to declare drought, announce relief of ₹40,000 for each farmer
content: Sugarcane growers seek waiver of crop loans availed from nationalised, co-operative and private banks

title: Taj Mahal or ‘Tejo Mahalaya’? The legal tussle explained
content: In 2017, the Archaeological Survey of India stated unambiguously that the Taj was a 17th-century tomb. The technology used in its construction and design, including pietra dura, did not exist in pre-medieval days. It should have ended any vestiges of controversy.

title: Supreme Court to examine whether persons deleted from voter list in SIR can be denied welfare schemes
content: Congress leader Prasenjit Bose’s petition said only 38,000 of 34 lakh appeals have been heard so far, leaving lakhs excluded from welfare schemes and facing hardship

title: TMC MLA Madan Mitra attends Mamata camp's assembly protest days after joining rebel faction
content: Clarifying his presence at the protest, Madan Mitra said he had remained there purely as a matter of courtesy after emerging from Ritabrata Banerjee's chamber in the Assembly, and insisted that it did not signify any change in his political position.

title: Mettur and Vaigai among 86 dams in Tamil Nadu to come under the scanner of independent panels of experts
content: The first comprehensive dam safety assessment for each dam should be carried out on or before December 30, 2026

title: TV journalist Vijayan quizzed by Chennai police in case on alleged attempt to bribe TVK MLA
content: Investigators found he had allegedly been in contact with the prime accused during the purported conspiracy

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Sonam Wangchuk dehydrated; fasting student leaders advised not to walk
content: Delhi HC to hear PIL on July 20 over 'continuous, intrusive' police surveillance at Jantar Mantar protest

title: Ram temple donation embezzlement: SIT to submit interim report on Monday
content: Sources further said the SIT may seek more time from the Uttar Pradesh government to finalise its investigation into the alleged financial irregularities related to donations received by the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust.

title: Kerala government begins process to convert CET into KTU constituent college, forms working group
content: The working group, constituted by the Higher Education department, is tasked with preparing a detailed proposal and order to transfer personnel, assets and liabilities

title: Kerala Knowledge Valley: Government kick-starts process to identify locations, establish linkages
content: The initiative aims to transform Kerala into a global higher education hub, bringing top-tier foreign universities and premier Indian institutions together in a single ecosystem

title: How musicians are reinventing the centuries-old abhang
content: Through multimedia presentations, fusion bands and digital platforms, musicians are finding new ways to take the works of saint poets to contemporary audiences.

title: Defence estate officers take possession of Union Gymkhana in Belagavi
content: This follows a court orders for eviction of a local group that was occupying 6.50 acres of land in the military cantonment area. The lease for the land had expired in 2002

title: Tamil Nadu Cabinet discusses on Budget
content: Law Minister Nirmalkumar slams Opposition over corruption and also alleged that the DMK, AIADMK and BJP are attempting to form a government together.

title: Tamil Nadu’s DMK MPs urge Centre to constitute tribunal for Mekedatu
content: “The Mettur Dam could not be opened because of Karnataka’s recalcitrant stand. This has caused enormous hardship to farmers,” a resolution adopted at the meeting said

title: SC extends Bengaluru civic polls to December due to Karnataka SIR
content: The plea had cited “severe logistical challenges” due to the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) in Karnataka

title: Sonam Wangchuk loses over 9 kg, weakens significantly on 19th day of hunger strike: CJP
content: Sonam Wangchuk remains under constant medical supervision, the CJP said in a statement, adding that he is still ‘mentally alert’, even as he has weakened significantly

title: Kerala Waqf Board reconstitution: Pinarayi Vijayan accuses UDF govt of ‘surrendering to Sangh Parivar diktats’
content: Opposition Leader alleges that State govt has ‘betrayed' Kerala’s secular society and minorities by deciding to reconstitute Waqf Board in accordance with Union government’s Waqf (Amendment) Act

title: India’s Quantum Leap: From Ancient Philosophy to Post-Quantum Cryptography
content: Read this essential guide to demystify the complex world of quantum computing and learn how India is navigating the paradigm shift.

title: Kerala Revenue Minister says State aims to ensure title deed for all eligible individuals
content: Minister says about 5,000 title deeds will be distributed across the State as part of UDF government’s 100-day action plan

title: Oil marketing companies yet to issue new LPG connections to domestic consumers in Chennai
content: Many say they have been waiting since March, when the conflict began in West Asia. They complain that cooking with induction stoves is not always convenient for them

title: NEET re-test results 2026: 12 students from Tamil Nadu score above 690
content: This year’s NEET results have indicated an increase in cut-off marks to the tune of about 30 to 35, compared to last year.

title: Parliamentary panel on bill to remove PM, CMs defers adoption of report
content: The Joint Committee on the 130th Amendment Bill had made five recommendations in its draft report, which was circulated to members recently

title: Patna High Court ruling on attempt to rape reignites debate, activists seek judges' training
content: The remarks came after the Patna High Court, according to media reports, held that attempting to remove a woman's salwar and pressing her breasts did not amount to an attempt to rape

title: ‘Merciless, ruthless, insensitive’: SC slams doctor for failure to urgently treat four-year-old rape victim
content: Supreme Court questioned the doctor’s medical ethics and warned the hospital that if it were to impose a monetary penalty for its conduct, it would have a chilling effect

title: Minister downplays risks from ‘data breach’ at Kudankulam nuclear plant
content: Speaking to The Hindu, Union MoS Jitendra Singh says the breach has nothing to do with nuclear security at the Kudankulam nuclear power plant; experts also do not seem worried by the incident

title: Watch | Thousands brave torrential rain to witness iconic Aanayoottu ritual at temple in Kerala’s Thrissur
content: 49 elephants feast under dark monsoon skies as devotees brave torrential rain to witness Thrissur’s iconic Karkkidakam ritual

title: S. Janaki (1938-2026) — A collection recalling the singer's pan-India career
content: S. Janaki won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for “Senthoora Poove” from 16 Vayathinile, the debut directorial venture of Bharathiraja, who passed away last month

title: Collector asks medical and health department officials to monitor seasonal diseases
content: Mosquito breeding centers needed to be removed to prevent spread of viral fevers with every Friday to be observed as a ‘Dry Day’ says, Collector Swapnil Dinakar Pundkar

title: Meghalaya Assembly to move resolution against uranium mining
content: Various groups have been mounting pressure on the Conrad K. Sangma government after Minister of State for Atomic Energy Jitendra Singh said that uranium mining in Meghalaya is still under examination

title: Which day — July 18 or November 1 — should be celebrated as the formation day of Tamil Nadu?
content: PMK leader Dr. Anbumani Ramadoss demands that November 1 be celebrated as Tamil Nadu Day. What is the reason behind the debate?

title: Why the numbers matter in the renewed push for  delimitation | Explained
content: For the Opposition, the immediate objective is to keep the NDA below the 360-vote threshold; even if the ruling alliance emerges as the single largest bloc by a comfortable margin, failing to secure two-thirds support will once again prevent the Constitution amendment from being passed.

title: July 23 is last date to apply for MBBS, BDS courses in Tamil Nadu
content: There will not be any extension for the last date for applications, the Tamil Nadu Selection Committee said.

title: Beyond momo: Meghalaya’s chefs are reclaiming the state’s culinary identity
content: In Shillong, chefs behind A’Origins, Rynsan and Lady Aiko are reclaiming Meghalaya’s culinary heritage with dishes built around fermented bamboo shoots, smoked meats, forest produce and traditional cooking techniques.

title: 13 students suspended for ragging, assaulting first-year student at Kerala college
content: Alleged incident happened in Government College Kasaragod. According to complainant, a group of senior students threatened and assaulted him on the campus on July 13

title: Illegal mosques, madrassas demolished near India-Pakistan border in Jaisalmer
content: According to the administration, the demolished structures had been built on government land, including land reserved for families displaced by the Pong Dam project

title: Rath Yatra: Pulling of chariots resumes amid chants of 'Jay Jagannath'
content: Special arrangements were made to drain rainwater from the Grand Road and facilitate the procession, as devotees were pulling the chariots of Lord Jagannath

title: Kerala power cuts: Pinarayi Vijayan accuses UDF govt of poor planning
content: Kerala’s Leader of the Opposition says LDF government was able to avoid such situations in the past decade by taking necessary steps in advance

title: Madras High Court omits names of TVK leaders Vijay, ‘Bussy’ Anand from writ petition on appointment of law officers
content: Justice Mohammed Shaffiq directs High Court Registry to drop their names since the petitioner’s counsel agreed not to press the case against them

title: Kafir screenshot case in Kerala: Court rejects SIT’s plea to cancel bail of prime accused Jithin Bhaskaran
content: The prosecution’s argument is that, after being released on bail on July 3, Jithin Bhaskaran violated bail conditions by posting messages on Facebook that allegedly challenged the investigating team

title: Nagercoil Sabari Varman custodial death: Family to receive body only after experts review autopsy report
content: When Ministers S. Rajesh Kumar and Srinath had met them to hand over the financial assistance on July 16, the family refused to accept the financial assistance.

title: Dates with devotion: The division in Puri’s Rath Yatra celebrations
content: The Jagannath Temple in Puri holds its Rath Yatra every year on a date prescribed by its centuries-old ritual calendar. A branch of ISKCON, however, stages international events of the procession on dates that differ from the original, citing practical reasons. The divergence has sparked a controversy, with many in Puri viewing it as a challenge to the temple’s authority and its religious identity, finds Satyasundar Barik

title: Supreme Court to hear appeal against ban on animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath for not being true to ‘Skanda Purana’
content: In an oral mentioning, senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, representing the film’s makers, Ele Animations, said the movie was due for release on July 17 in 300-odd theatres across the country

title: NEET-UG 2026 | A collection of explainers and expert opinions
content: The NEET-UG 2026 ‘paper leak’, and the structural inefficiencies that caused it, explained by The Hindu

title: PM Modi flags off India’s first hydrogen-powered train from Haryana’s Jind
content: The Prime Minister also laid the foundation stone of various development projects worth around ₹14,700 crore in Jind.

title: Ahead of U.S. Ambassador arrival, Trump okays 500% tariff Bill on Russian oil; withdraws from India-led ISA
content: U.S. Ambassador-designate Sergio Gor, who takes charge on January 12, 2026 has said ending India’s Russian oil imports is a “top priority”

title: How the new U.S. visa rules affect your online privacy | Explained
content: Privacy experts have slammed the U.S. government’s new social media vetting requirements, likening the country’s approach to that of an authoritarian regime. However, they note it is difficult to claim that privacy laws are being broken as visa applicants can choose to ignore the request, though they put their visa application at risk by doing so

title: Supreme Court allows release of animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath after Rath Yatra, says animation cannot erode devotion
content: The Bench took the middle path by allowing the movie to be released, but only after the completion of the ongoing Lord Jagannath Rath Yatra on July 27, 2026. The court directed that the film could be released on or after July 28.

title: Delhi HC to hear PIL alleging ‘intrusive police surveillance’ of Cockroach Janta Party protesters
content: The plea seeks a declaration that blanket photography, videography and operation of a surveillance tower at the protest site are unconstitutional

title: Train collides with school van in West Bengal's Murshidabad, at least three dead
content: Two of the three deceased are minors; the injured survivors have admitted to Murshidabad Medical College and Hospital

title: Delhi HC asks police to respond on bail plea by Sharjeel Imam in UAPA case
content: A Bench of Justice Prathiba M Singh and Justice Vikas Mahajan issued notice on Mr. Imam’s appeal challenging the trial court’s July 4 decision rejecting his second regular bail application; the court posted the case for hearing on August 27.

title: Punjab Paddy Yields At Risk Amid 28% Rain Deficit, Rise In Cultivation Costs
content: The increased dependence on groundwater has raised the cost of farming, with growers spending more on irrigation, fertilisers and crop protection chemicals to keep their fields healthy.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Closes 964 Points Higher, Tech Stocks Rally
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Oil prices jump nearly 12% this week as US-Iran strikes fuel Gulf supply fears.

title: Mehbooba Mufti Jabs Centre Over Sonam Wangchuk Protest, BJP Hits Back
content: Taking a swipe at the central governing for refusing to engage with the Cockroach Janta Party protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, the PDP chief said that British may have left but "colonial mindset seems to endure".

title: Why Skyroot's Vikram-1 Launch Could Be A Turning Point For India
content: If all goes according to plan, Skyroot's Vikram-1 rocket will become the first privately developed Indian rocket to attempt an orbital mission.

title: Wild Elephant Terrorising Andhra Villages Captured After Month-Long Hunt
content: The elephant, estimated to be 15-18 years old, had repeatedly strayed into human habitations over the past several months, damaging agricultural fields and creating panic among residents.

title: US Immigration Officer In Maine Shooting Has History Of Violent Behaviour: Report

title: 57 Hearings And An Unanswered Call For Affordable Drugs: Court Seeks Reply
